behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/aggregation. A fake diff can miss real `git` rename semantics (thresholds/status formats), so mocking can hide double-counting bugs. Rejected lower level (Integration) because behavior text is about repository-derived rename outcomes, not only in-memory categorization. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit baseline, then rename a file and run the CLI against actual repo state. Assert report shows the file only in `renamed`, with `added` and `deleted` unchanged for that path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test requires real git subprocess + repo state to expose rename classification failures.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk usually appears when reading/parsing real git diff output for binary blobs (encoding/patch format/process output). In-process mocks often omit exact binary diff behavior and would hide real failures. Rejected Integration due real subprocess/output-format dependency. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, add and modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI, and assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file changes in counts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses real git + filesystem binary content, matching the actual failure boundary.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for an unborn `HEAD`/no-commit repo and actual CLI error handling of subprocess exit/status/output. A mock may not reproduce exact failure modes. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly targets repository state that triggers real git command responses. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero exit (or documented non-error exit), and structured empty report instead of traceback/error text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real git repo state is necessary to catch no-commit edge handling bugs.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real commits/renames/deletes/modifies and actual git query behavior. Mocked history adapters can mask command/format mismatches. Rejected Integration because behavior validates truth against git itself. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on AI outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known sequence (add/delete/rename/modify across commits), run CLI for target range, and assert category totals exactly equal independently computed ground truth from git commands.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real history + CLI execution to validate full counting contract.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Difference between index and working tree is a real git state boundary; failures arise from how CLI invokes git against staged vs unstaged diffs. In-memory stubs typically do not model index/worktree nuances. Rejected Integration because behavior depends on real git staging mechanics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model involvement.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent changes once as unstaged and once staged (reset between runs), execute CLI in both states, and assert normalized stdout reports are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real filesystem + git index/worktree behavior to catch state-dependent output divergence.
